up.method: specific consumption rate calculated from measured biomass, residual glucose, O2/CO2 off-gas at steady state (D=0.039 h-1); numeric value in Supplementary Material, not in provided XML
measured from off-gas O2 content at steady state; numeric value not in provided text
measured NH4+ consumption at steady state; numeric value not in provided text
measured PO4 consumption at steady state; numeric value not in provided text

sec.method: measured from off-gas CO2 content at steady state; numeric value not in provided text
concentration measured during process; rate not tabulated in provided text

mfa.note: FBA-PREDICTED growth rate with m_iMK1208 model constrained by experimental chemostat values; agrees with experimental D=0.039 h-1 (validation). This is a model prediction, not a measured flux.

yields.Yxs: not tabulated in provided text (biomass and residual glucose measured at steady state)

growth_records.cite: Menendez-Bravo, Simón et al. Identification of FadAB Complexes Involved in Fatty Acid β-Oxidation in Streptomyces coelicolor and Construction of a Triacylglycerol Overproducing strain. Frontiers in Microbiology (2017). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2017.01428. [PMC5539140]
growth_records.method: calculated: at chemostat steady state mu=D=0.039 h-1 (experimental); doubling_time td=ln2/0.039=17.77 h. FBA-predicted growth using experimental constraints was 0.042 h-1 (in silico, not experimental)
growth_records.snippet: we carried out chemostat cultures of S. coelicolor wild type strain at a dilution rate of 0.039 h−1... concentrations of biomass, glucose, actinorhodin, NH4+, PO4... O2 and CO2 content in the outcoming gases... consumption rates were calcul
growth_records.notes: Glucose-limited aerobic chemostat, S. coelicolor M145 WT, Modified Evans medium, D=0.039 h-1, steady state mu=D=0.039 h-1 (experimental). td=ln2/0.039=17.77 h. The authors measured and calculated specific consumption rates of glucose, O2, CO2, NH4+, PO4 to constrain the FBA model, but the numeric rate values are not present in the main-text/methods XML (reported in Supplementary Material). Reported: pH 6.8±0.2, 30 C, 1000 rpm, 1.25 L/min air. FBA-predicted growth 0.042 h-1 is a model output (m_iMK1208), listed under mfa_fluxes as prediction, NOT experimental. Batch rich-medium (R5/90 h) TAG content was 11.2±0.02 ug TAG/mg DCW for M145, but no growth rate given for that condition.

up.method: specific uptake rate from Table 1 (calc per Table S7)
unit conversion of 0.08 g/gDW/h: 0.08/180.16*1000 = 0.444 mmol/gDW/h (glucose MW 180.16)
volumetric consumption rate from Table 1 (calc per Table S7)

yields.Yxs: 0.39 g biomass/g glucose (Table 1; footnote: biomass accumulation referenced to consumed glucose)

growth_records.cite: Kheirkhah, Tolue et al. Co-cultivation dynamics of the filamentous microorganisms Aspergillus niger and Streptomyces coelicolor in shake flasks. Microbial Cell Factories (2026). DOI:10.1186/s12934-026-03015-9. [PMC13154669]
growth_records.snippet: S. coelicolor from pellets (pre-culture) | NB | 0.14 ± 0.00 [µ, h-1] | 0.29 ± 0.02 [g (L h)-1] | 0.08 ± 0.00 [g (g h)-1] | N/A | N/A | 0.39 ± 0.06 [g g-1 Yx/s]
growth_records.notes: Table 1 row 'S. coelicolor from pellets (pre-culture)', axenic (only pure-prokaryote culture in the paper). µ=0.14 h-1 reported; doubling time td=ln2/µ=0.693/0.14=4.95 h (calculated). Yx/s=0.39 g/g reported. Column interpretation: Table 1 has after µ two [g (L h)-1] (volumetric) and two [g (g h)-1] (specific) columns plus Yx/s [g g-1]; process params 'calculated as described in Table S7' (not reproduced in main text). Inferred that the first volumetric+specific pair (0.29, 0.08) is glucose consumption and the second pair (N/A here) is L-glutamic acid consumption; glutamate columns are N/A for the inoculation-workflow experiments, consistent with only 1.5 g/L glutamate and glucose-referenced Yx/s. Glucose specific uptake 0.08 g/gDW/h -> 0.444 mmol/gDW/h (MW 180.16). Temperature 30 C, 250 rpm; pH adjusted to 6.0 or 7.0 depending on experiment (not disambiguated for this row -> pH null). SSBM-P salt base composition not given in text (ref 32) so medium composition is partial. Co-culture rows (A. niger + S. coelicolor) and A. niger-axenic rows (glutamic-acid-concentration series) were NOT emitted: co-culture µ/rates are for lumped fungal+bacterial biomass and cannot be attributed to the prokaryote alone; A. niger is a eukaryote.

growth_records.cite: Dethlefsen et al. Differences in codon bias cannot explain differences in translational power among microbes. BMC Bioinformatics (2005). DOI:10.1186/1471-2105-6-3. [PMC546186]
growth_records.notes: Table 1. Temp-corrected-to-37C mu (col3)=0.54 hr-1; actual temp 30C; factor 1.80. Actual mu = 0.54/1.80 = 0.300 hr-1; td=2.31 h. rrn copy#=6. Cited from ref [40]; secondary. No uptake/secretion/medium.

growth_records.cite: Kanhaiya Kumar et al. Nutrient-depended metabolic switching during batch cultivation of Streptomyces coelicolor explored with absolute quantitative mass spectrometry-based metabolite profiling. 3 Biotech (2022). DOI:10.1007/s13205-022-03146-x. [PMC8882213]
growth_records.snippet: SSBM-E media had a similar composition as SSBM-P media except for a reduced amount of Sodium L-glutamate monohydrate (16.6 g L-1), and an increased amount of phosphate (9.2 mM)... SSBM-E supported a slightly higher growth rate (0.21 +/- 0.0
growth_records.notes: Table 1 exponential-phase values: mu=0.21+/-0.04 h^-1 (from slope of ln[x] vs time); qGlut=0.03+/-0.02, qGlu(glucose)=0.02+/-0.01 mol/mol DCW/h; stationary qGlu=0.01+/-0.01. Doubling time = ln2/0.21 = 3.30 h. Unit conversion using stated biomass MW 27.0 g/mol: mmol/gDW/h = q(mol/mol/h) x 1000/27 = q x 37.0 (e.g. 0.02 -> 0.74; 0.03 -> 1.11). Pigment production rates are stationary-phase secretion (units umol/mol DCW/h). L-glutamate is both C and N source; its depletion = N limitation. RQ~1.1 exponential (ratio, not a rate). No numeric 13C net fluxes reported (only Rel SFL labeling).

growth_records.snippet: SSBM-P media consisted of D-glucose (40 g L-1), Sodium L-glutamate monohydrate (61.1 g L-1), Phosphate (4.6 mM), Magnesium sulfate (2.0 mM)... growth rate 0.16 +/- 0.01 h-1
growth_records.notes: Table 1 exponential values: mu=0.16+/-0.01 h^-1; qGlut=0.05+/-0.04, qGlu(glucose)=0.04+/-0.01 mol/mol DCW/h; stationary qGlut=0.02+/-0.01, qGlu=0.01+/-0.00. Doubling time = ln2/0.16 = 4.33 h. Conversion x37.0 (=1000/27 g/mol biomass): 0.04->1.48, 0.05->1.85. qGlut/qGlu higher in P lim than N lim despite lower mu. Carbon never limiting (glucose >10 g/L).

growth_records.notes: Table 1 M1146 P lim: mu=0.25+/-0.02 h^-1 (highest of the three cultures); qGlut=0.06+/-0.04, qGlu(glucose)=0.06+/-0.03 mol/mol DCW/h (expo); stationary both 0.01+/-0.01. Doubling time = ln2/0.25 = 2.77 h. Conversion x37.0: 0.06->2.22. No pigment secretion (BGCs deleted). Carbon recovery 78.4+/-5.2%.

growth_records.cite: Alexander Wentzel et al. Optimized submerged batch fermentation strategy for systems scale studies of metabolic switching in Streptomyces coelicolor A3(2). BMC Systems Biology (2012). DOI:10.1186/1752-0509-6-59. [PMC3431225]
growth_records.snippet: In three biological replicas cultivated in parallel at a constant agitation of 900 rpm (ferm. #14a-c) ... DOmin 12.7%, t(<40% DO) 21.2 h, mu_max 0.275 h-1, max CDW 8.17 g/L (Table 1)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 (ferm. #14a-c), organism S. coelicolor M145. mu_max=0.275 h-1 reported (from CDW time-course). Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.275=2.52 h. max CDW=8.17 g/L. Secondary-metabolite specific productivities (qPinit) are pigment absorbance-based 'spec. units', not molar. No substrate uptake numbers for this specific ferm. run. Medium SSBM-P from ref [19]; TMS1 sub-composition: FeSO4.7H2O 5 g/L, CuSO4.5H2O 390 mg/L, ZnSO4.7H2O 440 mg/L, MnSO4.H2O 150 mg/L, Na2MoO4.2H2O 10 mg/L, CoCl2.6H2O 20 mg/L, HCl 50 mL/L.

growth_records.cite: Alexander Wentzel et al. Optimized submerged batch fermentation strategy for systems scale studies of metabolic switching in Streptomyces coelicolor A3(2). BMC Systems Biology (2012). DOI:10.1186/1752-0509-6-59. [PMC3431225]
growth_records.snippet: the constant stirrer speed was increased to 1100 rpm (ferm. #15a-c) ... mu_max 0.298 h-1, max CDW 7.47 g/L, DOmin 12.0%, t(<40% DO) 12.2 h (Table 1)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 (ferm. #15a-c). mu_max=0.298 h-1 reported; td=0.693/0.298=2.33 h. max CDW=7.47 g/L. Same SSBM-P medium as ferm. #14a-c; only agitation (aeration) differs -> distinct condition.

growth_records.cite: Alexander Wentzel et al. Optimized submerged batch fermentation strategy for systems scale studies of metabolic switching in Streptomyces coelicolor A3(2). BMC Systems Biology (2012). DOI:10.1186/1752-0509-6-59. [PMC3431225]
growth_records.snippet: agitation was increased manually from 900 rpm to 1100 rpm after 21 h and further to 1300 rpm after 27 h ... (ferm. #16a-c) ... mu_max 0.260 h-1, max CDW 7.67 g/L, DOmin 38.7%, t(<40% DO) 0.7 h (Table 1)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 (ferm. #16a-c). mu_max=0.260 h-1 reported; td=0.693/0.260=2.67 h. max CDW=7.67 g/L. DO kept >~40% (DOmin 38.7). Distinct aeration regime on SSBM-P.

growth_records.cite: Alexander Wentzel et al. Optimized submerged batch fermentation strategy for systems scale studies of metabolic switching in Streptomyces coelicolor A3(2). BMC Systems Biology (2012). DOI:10.1186/1752-0509-6-59. [PMC3431225]
growth_records.snippet: an agitation regime where stirrer speed was adjusted automatically to maintain levels of minimum 50% DO with 325 rpm stirrer speed set as the minimum agitation (ferm. #17a-c) ... mu_max 0.254 h-1, max CDW 7.20 g/L (Table 1)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 (ferm. #17a-c). mu_max=0.254 h-1 reported; td=0.693/0.254=2.73 h. max CDW=7.20 g/L. Optimized 50% DO regime, adopted for the standard SSBM-P/SSBM-E high-resolution runs (#18/#19).

growth_records.medium: SSBM-P (phosphate depletion, high-resolution sampling run ferm. #18a)

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: PPP metabolites - summed fractional labelling from 13C6-glucose (companion 200 mL runs ferm #24/#25)
mfa.note: L-glutamate-derived carbon in PPP rises from 22% (growth phase) to 40% (secondary-metabolite phase); TCA cycle dominated by glutamate carbon, little 13C-glucose label reaches it. Qualitative label-distribution (summed fractional labelling), NOT quantitative net fluxes; no full 13C-MFA net-flux map computed.

growth_records.cite: Alexander Wentzel et al. Optimized submerged batch fermentation strategy for systems scale studies of metabolic switching in Streptomyces coelicolor A3(2). BMC Systems Biology (2012). DOI:10.1186/1752-0509-6-59. [PMC3431225]
growth_records.snippet: Table 2 Carbon consumption and derived secondary metabolite production yields in the time period 45-50 hrs. Ferm 18a: D-glucose 0.0059 Cmol/L-h, L-glutamate 0.0238 Cmol/L-h; Yields %Cmol RED 1.0, gamma-Act 0.4, TBP 1.0
growth_records.notes: Table 2, ferm. #18a (SSBM-P, phosphate depletion). Volumetric carbon-source consumption in the 45-50 h production phase (after phosphate depletion at ~35 h): D-glucose 0.0059 Cmol/L/h, L-glutamate 0.0238 Cmol/L/h. Molar conversion (glucose 6 C/mol): 0.0059/6=9.83e-4 mol/L/h=0.98 mmol glucose/L/h; (glutamate 5 C/mol): 0.0238/5=4.76e-3 mol/L/h=4.76 mmol glutamate/L/h. Approx specific rates using ~5 gDW/L biomass at that stage: glucose qS~0.98/5=0.20 mmol/gDW/h, glutamate qS~4.76/5=0.95 mmol/gDW/h (biomass ~5 g/L is approximate and declining during production phase, so specific values are estimates). L-glutamate consumed ~4x faster (Cmol basis) than glucose -> glutamate is the preferred C source. mu_max not tabulated for #18a but resembles #17a-c (~0.25 h-1). NOTE: this is production-phase (near-zero growth) consumption, not exponential-growth qS.

up.method: off-line enzymatic assay; volumetric consumption over 45-50 h (Table 2)
off-line L-glutamate Bioassay; glutamate already depleted (~35 h), zero consumption in 45-50 h window (Table 2)

sec.method: TBP dominates on SSBM-E; production much lower than SSBM-P; no molar rate
CO2 evolution rate collapses abruptly at L-glutamate depletion (on-line, no numeric value)

growth_records.cite: Alexander Wentzel et al. Optimized submerged batch fermentation strategy for systems scale studies of metabolic switching in Streptomyces coelicolor A3(2). BMC Systems Biology (2012). DOI:10.1186/1752-0509-6-59. [PMC3431225]
growth_records.snippet: Table 2 ... Ferm 19a: D-glucose 0.0083 Cmol/L-h, L-glutamate 0; Yields %Cmol RED 0.1, gamma-Act 2.2, TBP 4.9. 'at 35 h ... a maximum of 5 g/L CDW was reached'
growth_records.notes: Table 2, ferm. #19a (SSBM-E, L-glutamate depletion). In the 45-50 h window (post-glutamate-depletion), D-glucose consumed at 0.0083 Cmol/L/h; L-glutamate consumption = 0 (already depleted at ~35 h). Molar: 0.0083/6=1.38e-3 mol/L/h=1.38 mmol glucose/L/h. Note glucose consumption is HIGHER here than in SSBM-P production phase (0.0083 vs 0.0059 Cmol/L/h) once glutamate is gone. Max biomass ~5 g/L CDW at 35 h (text). SSBM-E differs from SSBM-P only in glutamate (15 vs 55.2 g/L) and phosphate (9.2 vs 4.6 mM). Growth ceases at glutamate depletion; D-glucose alone cannot sustain growth (proposed pyruvate->acetyl-CoA / pyruvate-dehydrogenase bottleneck).
